binds.tier: Tier 1 / Tier 1-adjacent (Court Filing, Arrest/Citation Records, Official Declarations)
binds.note: Court/arrest layer is staged from user-supplied payload. Specific filings, docket URLs, hashes, citations, and document line references remain unbound.

nps_court_declaration.named_official: Frank Lands, Deputy Director for Operations
reported_details: U.S. Park Police responded to NPS report of damage after rehabilitation/refilling.
Caulk over foam sealant reportedly cut with a sharp knife or razor.
Destruction of delaminating surface material reported.
Approximately 70 fence post tops reportedly thrown into the pool.

arrests_and_citations.caveat: Numbers have varied in prior statements; details on exact charges, individuals, and evidentiary linkage remain limited or unreleased.

notable_public_case.name: David Carter Hearn
notable_public_case.reported_position: Denies cutting or major damage; says he touched already-peeling liner material.

caveats: Formal detailed charges beyond the known public case are not fully bound in this receipt.
No comprehensive hashing or full release of photos/surveillance tied directly to the alleged knife-cut incident is bound here.
Some arrests/citations may relate to touching, peeling, or minor tampering rather than the primary alleged cut.
Causation remains unresolved: deliberate damage versus incidental interaction, renovation/coating failure, algae, water chemistry, or maintenance factors.

claim_effect.reason: Court/arrest layer strengthens enforcement-record coverage but does not yet bind full filings, hashes, surveillance, forensic photos, or causation closure.

open_questions_preserved: Scale of deliberate cuts versus renovation/coating failure.
Full release and hashing of photos, surveillance, court filings, administrative documents, and contractor reports.
Cost recovery and damage assessment under NPS Resource Protection protocols.
